1→# Metro Integrated Planning System (Monorepo)
     2→
     3→High-Performance Enterprise Integrated Planning System with "Semantic Zoom" UX (Google Earth-style).
     4→
     5→## Commands
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→bun i              # Install dependencies
     9→bun run dev        # Start development server
    10→bun run build      # Production build
    11→bun run typecheck  # Type check all packages
    12→bun run lint       # Lint all packages
    13→bun run test       # Run tests
    14→```
    15→
    16→## Tech Stack
    17→
    18→- **Runtime:** Bun
    19→- **Monorepo:** Turborepo
    20→- **Build Tool:** Vite 5.x
    21→- **Frontend:** React 18.3.1 (required for BlueprintJS 6)
    22→- **UI Components:** BlueprintJS 6.1.0 (Palantir)
    23→- **Graphics Engine:** @react-three/fiber 8.x, @react-three/drei 9.x, three 0.170.x
    24→- **State Management:** Custom state-machine (packages/toolkit)
    25→
    26→## Project Structure
    27→
    28→```
    29→system/
    30→├── apps/canvas/          # Production React app (Vite + R3F + BlueprintJS)
    31→├── packages/
    32→│   └── toolkit/          # State machine, utilities
    33→├── URGENT_POC/           # Archived POC (reference only)
    34→└── design/               # Visual reference specifications
    35→```
    36→
    37→## Visual References (design/)
    38→
    39→The `design/` directory contains the visual specifications that this system must replicate. See [design/README.md](./design/README.md) for detailed mapping between images and implementation layers.
    40→
    41→| Image | View | Description |
    42→|-------|------|-------------|
    43→| screen-1.png | Macro | Strategic 10-20 year heatmap overview |
    44→| screen-2.png | Meso | Tactical DNA-like allocation strips |
    45→| screen-3.jpg | Micro | Operational task cards with dependencies |
    46→| screen-4.png | Meso | Dense diagonal allocation pattern |
    47→| screen-5.png | Meso | Color-coded resource allocation |
    48→| screen-6.png | Micro | Task details with conflict indicators |
    49→| screen-7.png | UI | Header/sidebar layout reference |
    50→
    51→## LOD System (Level of Detail)
    52→
    53→The system implements three rendering layers based on camera zoom:
    54→
    55→- **Macro (zoom < 0.5):** Procedural shader heatmap - 1 draw call
    56→- **Meso (0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0):** InstancedMesh with 60k instances
    57→- **Micro (zoom > 2.0):** HTML overlay with Line2 dependency curves
    58→
    59→## Documentation
    60→
    61→- `docs/` - Domain documentation (requirements, UX specs, research)
    62→- `agent_docs/` - Technical documentation for AI agents
    63→- `design/` - Visual reference specifications
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→# Harness Protocol for Long-Running AI Agents
    68→
    69→## What is a Harness?
    70→
    71→A **harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work effectively on complex tasks that span multiple sessions. Created by **Anthropic** as part of the Claude Agent SDK (formerly Claude Code SDK), the harness addresses a fundamental challenge:
    72→
    73→> "The core challenge of long-running agents is that they must work in discrete sessions, and each new session begins with no memory of what came before."
    74→
    75→The harness acts as a **coordination layer** wrapped around ordinary Claude sessions. It tracks files, tasks, and test results across hours of execution, spinning up fresh conversations whenever one thread gets too bloated to stay coherent.
    76→
    77→## Why Anthropic Created the Harness
    78→
    79→After more than six months shipping updates to Claude Code, Anthropic solved hard problems:
    80→- How agents should manage **memory across long-running tasks**
    81→- How to handle **permission systems** that balance autonomy with user control
    82→- How to **coordinate subagents** working toward a shared goal
    83→
    84→The key insight was finding a way for agents to quickly understand the state of work when starting with a fresh context window.
    85→
    86→## Core Architecture
    87→
    88→```
    89→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    90→│                    Harness Framework                         │
    91→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    92→│                                                              │
    93→│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐  │
    94→│  │  Initializer │ → │    Coding    │ → │    Coding    │  │
    95→│  │    Agent     │    │    Agent     │    │    Agent     │  │
    96→│  │  (Session 1) │    │  (Session 2) │    │  (Session N) │  │
    97→│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘  │
    98→│         │                   │                   │           │
    99→│         └───────────────────┼───────────────────┘           │
   100→│                             │                               │
   101→│                             ▼                               │
   102→│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
   103→│  │              Shared State Files                       │  │
   104→│  │  • claude-progress.txt  (session history)            │  │
   105→│  │  • features.json        (source of truth)            │  │
   106→│  │  • CLAUDE.md            (project context)            │  │
   107→│  │  • git history          (code changes)               │  │
   108→│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
   109→│                                                              │
   110→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   111→```
   112→
   113→## Two-Agent Architecture
   114→
   115→Anthropic recommends a **two-fold approach**:
   116→
   117→### 1. Initializer Agent (First Session)
   118→- Sets up the initial environment
   119→- Creates the harness files (features.json, claude-progress.txt)
   120→- Establishes project structure
   121→- Runs on a specialized prompt optimized for setup
   122→
   123→### 2. Coding Agent (Subsequent Sessions)
   124→- Makes incremental progress on features
   125→- Leaves artifacts for the next session
   126→- Maintains clean code suitable for merging
   127→- Commits progress to git with descriptive messages
   128→
   129→## Core Files
   130→
   131→| File | Purpose |
   132→|------|---------|
   133→| `CLAUDE.md` | Project context, instructions, and conventions |
   134→| `claude-progress.txt` | Session history and current state |
   135→| `features.json` | Feature requirements with pass/fail status (source of truth) |
   136→| `agent_docs/` | Additional documentation for the agent |
   137→| `init.sh` | Script to start dev server and run basic tests |
   138→| `CLAUDE_NOTES.md` | Learnings and gotchas for future sessions |
   139→
   140→## The Work Loop
   141→
   142→When a session starts, the agent follows this loop:
   143→
   144→```
   145→1. READ claude-progress.txt
   146→   → Understand current state and what was done before
   147→
   148→2. READ features.json
   149→   → Identify next feature to implement (passes: false)
   150→
   151→3. RUN init.sh
   152→   → Start dev server, verify app is in working state
   153→
   154→4. IMPLEMENT one feature at a time
   155→   → Focus on single feature, don't try to do everything
   156→
   157→5. TEST the feature
   158→   → Verify it works as specified in steps
   159→
   160→6. COMMIT changes
   161→   → Granular commits with descriptive messages
   162→
   163→7. UPDATE features.json
   164→   → Mark feature as passes: true
   165→
   166→8. UPDATE claude-progress.txt
   167→   → Log what was done for next session
   168→
   169→9. REPEAT or STOP
   170→   → Continue to next feature or end session
   171→```
   172→
   173→## Critical AI Limitations & Mitigations
   174→
   175→> **IMPORTANT**: AI agents have fundamental limitations that this harness addresses.
   176→
   177→### The Blind Spot Problem
   178→
   179→**AI cannot see what it creates.** When building UI/apps, the AI:
   180→- Cannot see the rendered output
   181→- Often assumes blank screens are "working" because code compiled
   182→- Marks features as complete based on code logic, not visual verification
   183→- May claim success when the app shows nothing or errors
   184→
   185→**Solution**: Mandatory visual testing with MCP after every feature.
   186→
   187→### The Stale Knowledge Problem
   188→
   189→**AI training data is outdated.** Claude's knowledge cutoff means:
   190→- Library APIs may have changed
   191→- Configuration patterns may be deprecated
   192→- New versions have different syntax
   193→- Best practices may have evolved
   194→
   195→**Solution**: Always check the current date and search for updated documentation.
   196→
   197→### The Guessing Problem
   198→
   199→**AI tends to guess instead of research.** When stuck, AI will:
   200→- Try random solutions repeatedly
   201→- Waste context on failed attempts
   202→- Not realize when it's going in circles
   203→
   204→**Solution**: Mandatory research protocol for non-obvious errors.
   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→## Best Practices
   209→
   210→### 1. One Feature at a Time
   211→Don't attempt to complete everything at once. Focus on a single feature, test it, commit it, then move to the next.
   212→
   213→### 2. Clean State Between Sessions
   214→Leave the environment in a clean state:
   215→- No major bugs
   216→- Well-documented code
   217→- Orderly structure
   218→- Tests passing
   219→
   220→### 3. features.json is Source of Truth
   221→Never declare the project complete without all features passing. The `passes` field should only change from `false` to `true` after verification.
   222→
   223→### 4. Commit After Each Feature
   224→Maintain granular git history. Each feature should have its own commit(s).
   225→
   226→### 5. Research Before Guessing
   227→When encountering errors, classify them:
   228→- **Simple errors** (typos, syntax): Fix directly
   229→- **Complex errors** (runtime, config): Research online first
   230→
   231→### 6. Test Before Marking Complete
   232→Always verify the feature works before marking `passes: true`.
   233→
   234→### 7. ALWAYS Check Current Date
   235→Before starting work, verify the current date. **Do NOT assume you are in the year of your training data.** Use the system date to ensure you search for current documentation.
   236→
   237→### 8. Install Dependencies via Commands
   238→**NEVER manually edit package.json/Cargo.toml/requirements.txt to add dependencies.**
   239→
   240→```bash
   241→# CORRECT - Let package manager resolve versions
   242→bun add react
   243→npm install express
   244→pip install requests
   245→cargo add serde
   246→
   247→# WRONG - Manual version specification
   248→# Editing package.json: "react": "^18.0.0"  <- DON'T DO THIS
   249→```
   250→
   251→Why: Package managers install the latest stable compatible versions and handle peer dependencies correctly.
   252→
   253→---
   254→
   255→## MCP Visual Testing (MANDATORY)
   256→
   257→### Why Visual Testing is Required
   258→
   259→AI agents **cannot see rendered UI**. This creates a critical gap:
   260→
   261→```
   262→What AI knows:          What actually happens:
   263→✓ Code compiled         ✗ Blank white screen
   264→✓ No TypeScript errors  ✗ React error boundary
   265→✓ Tests passed          ✗ Component doesn't render
   266→✓ Server running        ✗ CSS completely broken
   267→```
   268→
   269→**Without visual verification, AI will mark broken features as complete.**
   270→
   271→### Visual Testing Protocol
   272→
   273→**See CLAUDE.md section "MCP VISUAL TESTING (MANDATORY)"** for the complete protocol.
   274→
   275→Summary: Use **Playwright MCP** (headless, does not steal focus):
   276→
   277→```
   278→1. mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="..."
   279→2. mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages level="error"
   280→3. mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot filename="..."
   281→4. Analyze screenshot
   282→5. mcp__playwright__browser_close
   283→```
   284→
   285→---
   286→
   287→## Error Research Protocol
   288→
   289→### When to Research vs. Fix Directly
   290→
   291→| Error Type | Example | Action |
   292→|------------|---------|--------|
   293→| **Obvious** | Typo in variable name | Fix directly |
   294→| **Syntax** | Missing semicolon | Fix directly |
   295→| **Import** | Wrong import path | Fix directly |
   296→| **Config** | Webpack/Vite config | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
   297→| **Runtime** | Module not found | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
   298→| **Library** | API changed | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
   299→| **Unknown** | Cryptic error | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
   300→
   301→### The STOP-SEARCH-UNDERSTAND-APPLY Method
   302→
   303→When encountering non-obvious errors:
   304→
   305→```
   306→1. STOP
   307→   - Do NOT try random solutions
   308→   - Do NOT keep making changes hoping something works
   309→   - Recognize when you're stuck
   310→
   311→2. SEARCH
   312→   - WebSearch: "[error message] + [technology] + [current year]"
   313→   - WebFetch: Read official documentation
   314→   - Include the CURRENT YEAR in searches (not your training year)
   315→
   316→3. UNDERSTAND
   317→   - Read the search results carefully
   318→   - Identify the ROOT CAUSE
   319→   - Understand WHY the error occurs
   320→
   321→4. APPLY
   322→   - Implement the CORRECT solution
   323→   - Not a workaround or hack
   324→   - The solution from documentation
   325→
   326→5. DOCUMENT
   327→   - Add to CLAUDE_NOTES.md if it's a recurring issue
   328→   - Note in claude-progress.txt
   329→```
   330→
   331→### Example: Research Flow
   332→
   333→```
   334→Error: "Module '@tauri-apps/api/fs' not found"
   335→
   336→❌ WRONG approach:
   337→   → Try different import paths randomly
   338→   → Downgrade versions blindly
   339→   → Copy code from training data (outdated)
   340→
   341→✅ CORRECT approach:
   342→   1. STOP - This is a config/library error
   343→   2. SEARCH - "tauri 2.0 fs module not found 2025"
   344→   3. UNDERSTAND - Tauri 2.0 moved fs to plugin system
   345→   4. APPLY - Install @tauri-apps/plugin-fs, update imports
   346→   5. DOCUMENT - Note in CLAUDE_NOTES.md
   347→```
   348→
   349→---
   350→
   351→## Session Startup Sequence
   352→
   353→Every coding agent session should begin with:
   354→
   355→```bash
   356→# 1. Confirm working directory
   357→pwd
   358→
   359→# 2. Read progress to understand state
   360→cat claude-progress.txt
   361→
   362→# 3. Check recent git history
   363→git log --oneline -10
   364→
   365→# 4. Read feature requirements
   366→cat features.json
   367→
   368→# 5. Start development server
   369→./init.sh
   370→
   371→# 6. Verify app is working
   372→# (run basic tests or visual check)
   373→
   374→# 7. Begin work on next feature
   375→```
   376→
   377→## Context Management
   378→
   379→The Claude Agent SDK implements automatic **compaction** when context limit approaches. However, you can help by:
   380→
   381→- Using `/clear` between unrelated tasks
   382→- Delegating research to subagents
   383→- Keeping CLAUDE.md concise
   384→- Writing clear, focused commit messages
   385→
   386→## Results
   387→
   388→Using this harness approach, Anthropic has observed:
   389→- Claude maintaining focus for **30+ hours** on complex tasks
   390→- Agents completing multi-week projects across sessions
   391→- Consistent quality without context degradation
   392→
   393→## Official Sources
   394→
   395→- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
   396→- [Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)
   397→- [Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)
   398→

# Metro Integrated Planning System (Monorepo)

High-Performance Enterprise Integrated Planning System with "Semantic Zoom" UX (Google Earth-style).

## Commands

```bash
bun i              # Install dependencies
bun run dev        # Start development server
bun run build      # Production build
bun run typecheck  # Type check all packages
bun run lint       # Lint all packages
bun run test       # Run tests
```

## Tech Stack

- **Runtime:** Bun
- **Monorepo:** Turborepo
- **Build Tool:** Vite 5.x
- **Frontend:** React 18.3.1 (required for BlueprintJS 6)
- **UI Components:** BlueprintJS 6.1.0 (Palantir)
- **Graphics Engine:** @react-three/fiber 8.x, @react-three/drei 9.x, three 0.170.x
- **State Management:** Custom state-machine (packages/toolkit)

## Project Structure

```
system/
├── apps/canvas/          # Production React app (Vite + R3F + BlueprintJS)
├── packages/
│   └── toolkit/          # State machine, utilities
├── URGENT_POC/           # Archived POC (reference only)
└── design/               # Visual reference specifications
```

## Visual References (design/)

The `design/` directory contains the visual specifications that this system must replicate. See [design/README.md](./design/README.md) for detailed mapping between images and implementation layers.

| Image | View | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| screen-1.png | Macro | Strategic 10-20 year heatmap overview |
| screen-2.png | Meso | Tactical DNA-like allocation strips |
| screen-3.jpg | Micro | Operational task cards with dependencies |
| screen-4.png | Meso | Dense diagonal allocation pattern |
| screen-5.png | Meso | Color-coded resource allocation |
| screen-6.png | Micro | Task details with conflict indicators |
| screen-7.png | UI | Header/sidebar layout reference |

## LOD System (Level of Detail)

The system implements three rendering layers based on camera zoom:

- **Macro (zoom < 0.5):** Procedural shader heatmap - 1 draw call
- **Meso (0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0):** InstancedMesh with 60k instances
- **Micro (zoom > 2.0):** HTML overlay with Line2 dependency curves

## Documentation

- `docs/` - Domain documentation (requirements, UX specs, research)
- `agent_docs/` - Technical documentation for AI agents
- `design/` - Visual reference specifications

---

# Harness Protocol for Long-Running AI Agents

## What is a Harness?

A **harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work effectively on complex tasks that span multiple sessions. Created by **Anthropic** as part of the Claude Agent SDK (formerly Claude Code SDK), the harness addresses a fundamental challenge:

> "The core challenge of long-running agents is that they must work in discrete sessions, and each new session begins with no memory of what came before."

The harness acts as a **coordination layer** wrapped around ordinary Claude sessions. It tracks files, tasks, and test results across hours of execution, spinning up fresh conversations whenever one thread gets too bloated to stay coherent.

## Why Anthropic Created the Harness

After more than six months shipping updates to Claude Code, Anthropic solved hard problems:
- How agents should manage **memory across long-running tasks**
- How to handle **permission systems** that balance autonomy with user control
- How to **coordinate subagents** working toward a shared goal

The key insight was finding a way for agents to quickly understand the state of work when starting with a fresh context window.

## Core Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Harness Framework                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐  │
│  │  Initializer │ → │    Coding    │ → │    Coding    │  │
│  │    Agent     │    │    Agent     │    │    Agent     │  │
│  │  (Session 1) │    │  (Session 2) │    │  (Session N) │  │
│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘  │
│         │                   │                   │           │
│         └───────────────────┼───────────────────┘           │
│                             │                               │
│                             ▼                               │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │              Shared State Files                       │  │
│  │  • claude-progress.txt  (session history)            │  │
│  │  • features.json        (source of truth)            │  │
│  │  • CLAUDE.md            (project context)            │  │
│  │  • git history          (code changes)               │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Two-Agent Architecture

Anthropic recommends a **two-fold approach**:

### 1. Initializer Agent (First Session)
- Sets up the initial environment
- Creates the harness files (features.json, claude-progress.txt)
- Establishes project structure
- Runs on a specialized prompt optimized for setup

### 2. Coding Agent (Subsequent Sessions)
- Makes incremental progress on features
- Leaves artifacts for the next session
- Maintains clean code suitable for merging
- Commits progress to git with descriptive messages

## Core Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | Project context, instructions, and conventions |
| `claude-progress.txt` | Session history and current state |
| `features.json` | Feature requirements with pass/fail status (source of truth) |
| `agent_docs/` | Additional documentation for the agent |
| `init.sh` | Script to start dev server and run basic tests |
| `CLAUDE_NOTES.md` | Learnings and gotchas for future sessions |

## The Work Loop

When a session starts, the agent follows this loop:

```
1. READ claude-progress.txt
   → Understand current state and what was done before

2. READ features.json
   → Identify next feature to implement (passes: false)

3. RUN init.sh
   → Start dev server, verify app is in working state

4. IMPLEMENT one feature at a time
   → Focus on single feature, don't try to do everything

5. TEST the feature
   → Verify it works as specified in steps

6. COMMIT changes
   → Granular commits with descriptive messages

7. UPDATE features.json
   → Mark feature as passes: true

8. UPDATE claude-progress.txt
   → Log what was done for next session

9. REPEAT or STOP
   → Continue to next feature or end session
```

## Critical AI Limitations & Mitigations

> **IMPORTANT**: AI agents have fundamental limitations that this harness addresses.

### The Blind Spot Problem

**AI cannot see what it creates.** When building UI/apps, the AI:
- Cannot see the rendered output
- Often assumes blank screens are "working" because code compiled
- Marks features as complete based on code logic, not visual verification
- May claim success when the app shows nothing or errors

**Solution**: Mandatory visual testing with MCP after every feature.

### The Stale Knowledge Problem

**AI training data is outdated.** Claude's knowledge cutoff means:
- Library APIs may have changed
- Configuration patterns may be deprecated
- New versions have different syntax
- Best practices may have evolved

**Solution**: Always check the current date and search for updated documentation.

### The Guessing Problem

**AI tends to guess instead of research.** When stuck, AI will:
- Try random solutions repeatedly
- Waste context on failed attempts
- Not realize when it's going in circles

**Solution**: Mandatory research protocol for non-obvious errors.

---

## Best Practices

### 1. One Feature at a Time
Don't attempt to complete everything at once. Focus on a single feature, test it, commit it, then move to the next.

### 2. Clean State Between Sessions
Leave the environment in a clean state:
- No major bugs
- Well-documented code
- Orderly structure
- Tests passing

### 3. features.json is Source of Truth
Never declare the project complete without all features passing. The `passes` field should only change from `false` to `true` after verification.

### 4. Commit After Each Feature
Maintain granular git history. Each feature should have its own commit(s).

### 5. Research Before Guessing
When encountering errors, classify them:
- **Simple errors** (typos, syntax): Fix directly
- **Complex errors** (runtime, config): Research online first

### 6. Test Before Marking Complete
Always verify the feature works before marking `passes: true`.

### 7. ALWAYS Check Current Date
Before starting work, verify the current date. **Do NOT assume you are in the year of your training data.** Use the system date to ensure you search for current documentation.

### 8. Install Dependencies via Commands
**NEVER manually edit package.json/Cargo.toml/requirements.txt to add dependencies.**

```bash
# CORRECT - Let package manager resolve versions
bun add react
npm install express
pip install requests
cargo add serde

# WRONG - Manual version specification
# Editing package.json: "react": "^18.0.0"  <- DON'T DO THIS
```

Why: Package managers install the latest stable compatible versions and handle peer dependencies correctly.

---

## MCP Visual Testing (MANDATORY)

### Why Visual Testing is Required

AI agents **cannot see rendered UI**. This creates a critical gap:

```
What AI knows:          What actually happens:
✓ Code compiled         ✗ Blank white screen
✓ No TypeScript errors  ✗ React error boundary
✓ Tests passed          ✗ Component doesn't render
✓ Server running        ✗ CSS completely broken
```

**Without visual verification, AI will mark broken features as complete.**

### Visual Testing Protocol

**See CLAUDE.md section "MCP VISUAL TESTING (MANDATORY)"** for the complete protocol.

Summary: Use **Playwright MCP** (headless, does not steal focus):

```
1. mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="..."
2. mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages level="error"
3. mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot filename="..."
4. Analyze screenshot
5. mcp__playwright__browser_close
```

---

## Error Research Protocol

### When to Research vs. Fix Directly

| Error Type | Example | Action |
|------------|---------|--------|
| **Obvious** | Typo in variable name | Fix directly |
| **Syntax** | Missing semicolon | Fix directly |
| **Import** | Wrong import path | Fix directly |
| **Config** | Webpack/Vite config | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
| **Runtime** | Module not found | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
| **Library** | API changed | **RESEARCH FIRST** |
| **Unknown** | Cryptic error | **RESEARCH FIRST** |

### The STOP-SEARCH-UNDERSTAND-APPLY Method

When encountering non-obvious errors:

```
1. STOP
   - Do NOT try random solutions
   - Do NOT keep making changes hoping something works
   - Recognize when you're stuck

2. SEARCH
   - WebSearch: "[error message] + [technology] + [current year]"
   - WebFetch: Read official documentation
   - Include the CURRENT YEAR in searches (not your training year)

3. UNDERSTAND
   - Read the search results carefully
   - Identify the ROOT CAUSE
   - Understand WHY the error occurs

4. APPLY
   - Implement the CORRECT solution
   - Not a workaround or hack
   - The solution from documentation

5. DOCUMENT
   - Add to CLAUDE_NOTES.md if it's a recurring issue
   - Note in claude-progress.txt
```

### Example: Research Flow

```
Error: "Module '@tauri-apps/api/fs' not found"

❌ WRONG approach:
   → Try different import paths randomly
   → Downgrade versions blindly
   → Copy code from training data (outdated)

✅ CORRECT approach:
   1. STOP - This is a config/library error
   2. SEARCH - "tauri 2.0 fs module not found 2025"
   3. UNDERSTAND - Tauri 2.0 moved fs to plugin system
   4. APPLY - Install @tauri-apps/plugin-fs, update imports
   5. DOCUMENT - Note in CLAUDE_NOTES.md
```

---

## Session Startup Sequence

Every coding agent session should begin with:

```bash
# 1. Confirm working directory
pwd

# 2. Read progress to understand state
cat claude-progress.txt

# 3. Check recent git history
git log --oneline -10

# 4. Read feature requirements
cat features.json

# 5. Start development server
./init.sh

# 6. Verify app is working
# (run basic tests or visual check)

# 7. Begin work on next feature
```

## Context Management

The Claude Agent SDK implements automatic **compaction** when context limit approaches. However, you can help by:

- Using `/clear` between unrelated tasks
- Delegating research to subagents
- Keeping CLAUDE.md concise
- Writing clear, focused commit messages

## Results

Using this harness approach, Anthropic has observed:
- Claude maintaining focus for **30+ hours** on complex tasks
- Agents completing multi-week projects across sessions
- Consistent quality without context degradation

## Official Sources

- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)
- [Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)

# Resume Work Instruction

**Role:** Senior Frontend Engineer specializing in WebGL and React.
**Project:** Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS).

## 🛑 STOP & READ FIRST
You are picking up an ongoing session. To restore context, you **MUST** read these files immediately:

1.  `./CLAUDE.md` - **READ THIS FIRST**. It contains the project rules, commands, and "Harness" protocol.
2.  `./claude-progress.txt` - The session log. Check the last entry to see exactly where we left off.
3.  `./features.json` - The detailed roadmap.
    -   **ACTION:** Find the first feature where `"passes": false` (likely **F016**). This is your SINGLE task for this turn.

## 🚀 Execution Protocol (The Harness)
Follow this exact sequence. Do not deviate.

1.  **Identify:** Confirm the next feature ID (e.g., F016).
2.  **Implement:** Execute the specific `steps` listed in `features.json` for that feature.
3.  **Verify (Visual):**
    -   **Rule:** You are "blind" to the UI. You **MUST** use visual testing (screenshots) to confirm the implementation works.
    -   Do not assume code works just because it compiles.
4.  **Commit:** `git commit -am "feat: complete [F-XXX] <description>"`
5.  **Update:** Mark `"passes": true` in `features.json` and append a log entry to `claude-progress.txt`.

## ⏭️ Immediate Next Step
Read the context files above. Then, explicitly state:
*"I have loaded the context. The next pending feature is **[F-XXX: Description]**. I will now begin step 1..."*

# Notes for Claude - Future Reference This file contains learnings, gotchas, and solutions discovered during development. Read this to avoid repeating past mistakes. --- ## Technology-Specific Notes ### React Three Fiber (R3F) #### Key Learning R3F v9 has significant API changes from v8. Always check the latest documentation. #### Common Pitfalls - Never use `useFrame` outside of Canvas context - `Html` component from drei requires `transform` prop for 3D positioning - InstancedMesh color changes require `needsUpdate = true` on the attribute #### Correct Usage ```typescript // Correct way to update InstancedMesh colors mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true; // Correct Html positioning in 3D
Content
``` --- ### Astro + React Integration #### Key Learning Astro's React integration requires specific hydration directives for client-only rendering. #### Important Configuration ```astro ``` #### Common Pitfalls - Don't use `client:load` for R3F - it will cause SSR issues - `client:only="react"` completely skips server rendering - Ensure React 19 compatibility with all libraries --- ### Three.js Shaders (GLSL) #### Key Learning Fragment shaders for heatmaps need careful color interpolation. #### Correct Usage ```glsl // Color ramp for heatmap (Yellow → Orange → Red) vec3 ramp(float t) { vec3 a = vec3(1.0, 0.95, 0.5); // light yellow vec3 b = vec3(1.0, 0.6, 0.0); // orange vec3 c = vec3(0.85, 0.0, 0.0); // red return mix(mix(a, b, smoothstep(0.0, 0.5, t)), c, smoothstep(0.5, 1.0, t)); } ``` --- ### Bun Workspaces / Monorepo #### Key Learning Bun workspaces require proper package.json structure and cross-package imports. #### Important Configuration ```json { "workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"] } ``` #### CRITICAL: Running Scripts in Monorepos **The root `bun run dev` may fail** if the filter doesn't match the package name: ```bash # Root package.json uses filter: "dev": "bun --bun run --filter '@mms/app-system' dev" # The filter MUST match the 'name' field in apps/system/package.json: "name": "@mms/app-system" # ← This is what the filter matches ``` **If root scripts fail, ALWAYS try the direct approach:** ```bash # This ALWAYS works: cd apps/system && bun run dev # Or check what the package is actually named: cat apps/system/package.json | grep '"name"' ``` #### Common Pitfalls - **Never assume root scripts work** - verify first with `./init.sh --no-server` - Root scripts use filters that must match exact package names - Always use `bun add` from the correct directory - Cross-package imports need proper exports in package.json - Run `bun i` from root after adding new packages --- ## Package/Library Notes ### @react-three/drei #### IMPORTANT: Html Component **Correct import**: `import { Html } from '@react-three/drei'` #### Why this matters The Html component bridges WebGL and DOM, allowing real HTML elements to be positioned in 3D space. #### Correct usage ```tsx
Clickable content
``` --- ### three-stdlib #### IMPORTANT: Line2 for Fat Lines **Correct import**: `import { Line2, LineGeometry, LineMaterial } from 'three-stdlib'` #### Why this matters Standard Three.js Line has 1px width limit. Line2 supports variable width. #### Correct usage ```typescript const geometry = new LineGeometry(); geometry.setPositions([x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, z2]); const material = new LineMaterial({ color: 0x3b82f6, linewidth: 2, resolution: new Vector2(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight) }); ``` --- ## Error Solutions ### Error: "No packages matched the filter" (Bun monorepo) **Cause**: Root script uses `--filter` with wrong package name **Solution**: 1. Check actual package name: `cat apps/system/package.json | grep '"name"'` 2. Run directly in the app folder: `cd apps/system && bun run dev` 3. Or fix the filter in root package.json to match the actual package name ### Error: "Cannot use import statement outside a module" **Cause**: Package trying to use CommonJS in ESM context **Solution**: Ensure `"type": "module"` in package.json, use proper imports ### Error: "useFrame must be called inside Canvas" **Cause**: React hook called outside R3F context **Solution**: Move component inside `` or pass state via props/context ### Error: "THREE.WebGLRenderer: Context Lost" **Cause**: GPU memory exhausted or too many textures **Solution**: Dispose unused textures, reduce DataTexture size, check for memory leaks ### Error: InstancedMesh colors not updating **Cause**: Buffer attribute not marked for update **Solution**: Set `mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true` after changing colors --- ## General Rules 1. **Always research official documentation** before trying to resolve import/ESM errors 2. **Check for alternative versions** of packages for different bundlers 3. **Don't remove dependencies** the user explicitly requested - find the correct way to use them 4. **Test after every change** - don't batch multiple changes 5. **Commit frequently** - small, focused commits are better than large ones 6. **Use bun add** - never manually edit package.json for dependencies --- ## Project-Specific Gotchas ### LOD State Machine The LOD (Level of Detail) is controlled by zoom level: - `zoom < 0.5` → 'macro' (shader heatmap) - `0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0` → 'meso' (InstancedMesh) - `zoom > 2.0` → 'micro' (HTML overlay) ### Virtual Scroll Sync Sidebar scroll is synced with camera Y using: ```typescript const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24; // DOM → Camera const y = -el.scrollTop / PX_PER_TRAIN; // Camera → DOM el.scrollTop = -viewport.y * PX_PER_TRAIN; ``` ### Spatial Hash Grid MesoLayer uses spatial hashing for O(1) instance picking: - Grid cell size matches task dimensions - Hash key: `${Math.floor(x / cellSize)}_${Math.floor(y / cellSize)}` --- ## Useful Commands ```bash # Install all workspace dependencies bun i # Start development server bun run dev # Type check all packages bun run typecheck # Build for production bun run build # Check git status git status # View recent commits git --no-pager log --oneline -10 ``` --- ## Resources - [React Three Fiber Docs](https://docs.pmnd.rs/react-three-fiber) - [Drei Docs](https://github.com/pmndrs/drei) - [Three.js Docs](https://threejs.org/docs/) - [Astro Docs](https://docs.astro.build) - [Bun Docs](https://bun.sh/docs) - [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/docs)

# Visual Design Specifications

This directory contains the **Pixel-Perfect Target** visual references for the Metro Integrated Planning System. Each image represents a specific view or component that must be replicated with high fidelity.

## View Mapping

### Macro View (Strategic Planning: 10-20 years)
**Implementation:** `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MacroLayer.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-1.png` | Dense grid/heatmap showing fleet density and "health" via color gradients (Yellow → Orange → Red) |

**Technical Requirements:**
- Single `PlaneGeometry` covering visible area
- Fragment Shader (GLSL) receiving `DataTexture`
- Color ramp: Yellow (low density) → Orange → Red (high density)
- Goal: 1 Draw Call, native GPU performance

---

### Meso View (Tactical Planning: 2-5 years)
**Implementation:** `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-2.png` | Colorful diagonal strips (DNA-like pattern) - high-density individual allocations |
| `screen-4.png` | Dense diagonal allocation pattern with interleaved colors |
| `screen-5.png` | Color-coded resource allocation blocks |

**Technical Requirements:**
- `InstancedMesh` with up to 60,000 instances
- Colors controlled via `InstancedBufferAttribute`
- Spatial Hash Grid for hover detection
- Color palette: Deep Blue (routine), Orange (overhaul)

---

### Micro View (Operational Planning: 4-8 weeks)
**Implementation:** `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MicroLayer.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-3.jpg` | Text boxes, alert icons, arrows showing task dependencies |
| `screen-6.png` | Task detail cards with conflict indicators (red alerts) |

**Technical Requirements:**
- `<Html>` component from drei with `transform` for task cards
- `Line2` (Fat Lines) for dependency arrows (smooth Bezier curves)
- Full Tailwind styling (shadows, rounded corners, flexbox)
- Accessibility: keyboard focus (tabIndex), native click events
- Conflict indicator: red border + warning icon

---

### UI Layout Reference
**Implementation:** `apps/system/src/components/PlanningView.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-7.png` | Header bar + left sidebar (Train IDs) layout |

**Technical Requirements:**
- Header: Fleet availability %, budget status
- Left sidebar: Scrollable train list synchronized with canvas Y position
- Footer: Time scrubber / Minimap controlling canvas X position

---

## Color Palette

| Element | Color | Hex |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Background | Soft corporate gray | `#f4f4f5` (zinc-100) |
| Task (routine) | Deep Blue | `#1d4ed8` (blue-700) |
| Task (overhaul) | Orange | `#f59e0b` (amber-500) |
| Alert/Conflict | Danger Red | `#dc2626` (red-600) |
| Dependency lines | Slate | `#64748b` (slate-500) |

---

## Implementation Checklist

- [x] MacroLayer: Procedural shader heatmap
- [x] MesoLayer: InstancedMesh with spatial hashing
- [x] MicroLayer: HTML overlay + Line2 curves
- [x] Virtual scroll sync (sidebar ↔ canvas)
- [x] Time scrubber minimap
- [ ] Pixel-perfect color matching
- [ ] Fine-tuning visual density patterns

1→# @belt/toolkit 2→ 3→A TypeScript utility library providing runtime type validation with full type inference, state management, and common helpers. 4→ 5→## Installation 6→ 7→```bash 8→pnpm add @belt/toolkit 9→``` 10→ 11→## Quick Start 12→ 13→```typescript 14→import { createType, createModel, createMachine, createSlice, ms, invariant } from '@belt/toolkit'; 15→ 16→// Define a validated type with full TypeScript inference 17→const UserSchema = createModel({ 18→ id: 'number', 19→ name: 'string', 20→ email: 'string?', 21→ roles: 'string[]', 22→}); 23→ 24→// Parse and validate data 25→const user = UserSchema.parse(data); // Throws if invalid 26→``` 27→ 28→--- 29→ 30→## Schema System 31→ 32→Runtime type validation with compile-time TypeScript inference. 33→ 34→### createType / createModel 35→ 36→```typescript 37→import { createType, createModel, InferType } from '@belt/toolkit'; 38→ 39→// String-based definition 40→const StringType = createType('string'); 41→const NumberArrayType = createType('number[]'); 42→const OptionalStringType = createType('string?'); 43→ 44→// Object schema with createModel (shorthand) 45→const UserSchema = createModel({ 46→ id: 'number', 47→ name: 'string', 48→ email: 'string?', 49→}); 50→ 51→// Equivalent using createType 52→const UserSchema2 = createType({ 53→ properties: { 54→ id: 'number', 55→ name: 'string', 56→ email: 'string?', 57→ }, 58→}); 59→ 60→// Get TypeScript type from schema 61→type User = InferType; 62→// => { id: number; name: string; email?: string } 63→``` 64→ 65→### Type Definitions 66→ 67→#### Primitive Types 68→ 69→| Definition | TypeScript Type | Description | 70→|------------|-----------------|-------------| 71→| `'string'` | `string` | String values | 72→| `'number'` | `number` | Numeric values (rejects NaN) | 73→| `'boolean'` | `boolean` | Boolean values | 74→| `'null'` | `null` | Null only | 75→| `'undefined'` | `undefined` | Undefined only | 76→| `'date'` | `Date` | Valid Date objects | 77→| `'function'` | `Function` | Any function | 78→| `'object'` | `object` | Plain objects | 79→| `'unknown'` | `unknown` | Any value (no validation) | 80→| `'any'` | `any` | Any value (no validation) | 81→ 82→#### Modifiers 83→ 84→| Modifier | Example | Result | 85→|----------|---------|--------| 86→| `?` (optional) | `'string?'` | `string \| undefined` | 87→| `[]` (array) | `'string[]'` | `string[]` | 88→| `[]?` (optional array) | `'string[]?'` | `string[] \| undefined` | 89→ 90→#### Object Definitions 91→ 92→```typescript 93→// Object with properties 94→createType({ 95→ properties: { 96→ name: 'string', 97→ age: 'number', 98→ email: 'string?', // optional 99→ }, 100→}); 101→ 102→// Nested objects 103→createType({ 104→ properties: { 105→ user: { 106→ properties: { 107→ name: 'string', 108→ address: { 109→ properties: { 110→ city: 'string', 111→ zip: 'string', 112→ }, 113→ }, 114→ }, 115→ }, 116→ }, 117→}); 118→``` 119→ 120→#### Enum Definitions 121→ 122→```typescript 123→import { stringEnum } from '@belt/toolkit'; 124→ 125→// Using stringEnum helper (recommended) 126→const StatusValues = stringEnum('pending', 'active', 'completed'); 127→const StatusSchema = createType({ enum: StatusValues }); 128→ 129→// Inline (requires 'as const') 130→const StatusSchema2 = createType({ 131→ enum: ['pending', 'active', 'completed'] as const, 132→}); 133→ 134→type Status = InferType; // => 'pending' | 'active' | 'completed' 135→``` 136→ 137→#### Union Definitions 138→ 139→```typescript 140→// Union of primitives 141→const StringOrNumber = createType({ 142→ union: ['string', 'number'], 143→}); 144→ 145→// Nullable type 146→const NullableString = createType({ 147→ union: ['string', 'null'], 148→}); 149→ 150→type Result = InferType; // => string | number 151→``` 152→ 153→#### Array Definitions 154→ 155→```typescript 156→// Simple array (string shorthand) 157→const StringArray = createType('string[]'); 158→ 159→// Array with items syntax 160→const StringArray2 = createType({ items: 'string' }); 161→ 162→// Array of objects 163→const UserList = createType({ 164→ items: { 165→ properties: { 166→ id: 'number', 167→ name: 'string', 168→ }, 169→ }, 170→}); 171→ 172→// Using .list() method 173→const UserSchema = createModel({ id: 'number', name: 'string' }); 174→const UserListSchema = UserSchema.list(); 175→``` 176→ 177→#### Record Definitions 178→ 179→```typescript 180→// Record 181→const StringRecord = createType({ record: 'string' }); 182→const NumberRecord = createType({ record: 'number' }); 183→ 184→type Dict = InferType; // => Record 185→``` 186→ 187→### Schema Methods 188→ 189→```typescript 190→const Schema = createModel({ 191→ id: 'number', 192→ name: 'string', 193→}); 194→ 195→// .parse(value, path?) - Validate and return typed value, throws on error 196→const user = Schema.parse({ id: 1, name: 'John' }); 197→ 198→// Parse from nested path 199→const user2 = Schema.parse(apiResponse, 'data.user'); 200→ 201→// .parseList(value, path?) - Parse array of items 202→const users = Schema.parseList([{ id: 1, name: 'John' }]); 203→ 204→// .isValid(value) - Type guard, returns boolean 205→if (Schema.isValid(data)) { 206→ // data is typed as { id: number; name: string } 207→} 208→ 209→// .list() - Get array schema (cached) 210→const ListSchema = Schema.list(); 211→``` 212→ 213→### Helper Functions 214→ 215→```typescript 216→import { isType, invariantType } from '@belt/toolkit'; 217→ 218→// isType - Type guard 219→const value: unknown = getData(); 220→if (isType(value, 'string')) { 221→ // value is string 222→} 223→if (isType(value, 'date')) { 224→ // value is Date 225→} 226→ 227→// invariantType - Assert or throw 228→function processUser(data: unknown) { 229→ invariantType('object', data); 230→ // data is now typed as object 231→} 232→ 233→// Custom error message with placeholders 234→invariantType('string', value, processUser, 235→ 'Expected $definition but got $typename: $originalMessage' 236→); 237→``` 238→ 239→### Type Inference 240→ 241→```typescript 242→import { InferType, Type } from '@belt/toolkit'; 243→ 244→const Schema = createModel({ 245→ id: 'number', 246→ name: 'string', 247→ status: { enum: ['active', 'inactive'] as const }, 248→ tags: 'string[]', 249→ metadata: 'object?', 250→}); 251→ 252→// Extract TypeScript type 253→type MyType = InferType; 254→// => { 255→// id: number; 256→// name: string; 257→// status: 'active' | 'inactive'; 258→// tags: string[]; 259→// metadata?: object; 260→// } 261→ 262→// Schema types are cached for string definitions 263→const a = createType('string'); 264→const b = createType('string'); 265→console.log(a === b); // true 266→``` 267→ 268→--- 269→ 270→## State Machine 271→ 272→Redux-like state management with slices, caching, and React hooks. 273→ 274→### createMachine 275→ 276→```typescript 277→import { createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '@belt/toolkit'; 278→ 279→const machine = createMachine({ 280→ id: 'app', 281→ slices: { 282→ counter: createSlice({ 283→ id: 'counter', 284→ initial: { count: 0 }, 285→ cache: 'session', 286→ reducers: { 287→ increment: (draft) => { draft.count += 1; }, 288→ decrement: (draft) => { draft.count -= 1; }, 289→ set: (draft, value: number) => { draft.count = value; }, 290→ }, 291→ }), 292→ user: createAsyncSlice({ 293→ id: 'user', 294→ cache: 'user', 295→ context: { userID: '' }, 296→ load: async (ctx) => { 297→ const res = await fetch(`/api/users/${ctx.userID}`); 298→ return res.json(); 299→ }, 300→ }), 301→ }, 302→ selectors: { 303→ doubleCount: (state) => state.counter.count * 2, 304→ }, 305→ context: { userID: 'user-123' }, 306→}); 307→``` 308→ 309→### createSlice (Sync) 310→ 311→```typescript 312→const counterSlice = createSlice({ 313→ id: 'counter', 314→ initial: { count: 0, history: [] as number[] }, 315→ cache: 'session', // 'session' | 'user' | 'global' 316→ reducers: { 317→ // Reducers receive Immer draft - mutate directly 318→ increment: (draft) => { 319→ draft.count += 1; 320→ }, 321→ add: (draft, amount: number) => { 322→ draft.count += amount; 323→ draft.history.push(draft.count); 324→ }, 325→ reset: (draft) => { 326→ draft.count = 0; 327→ draft.history = []; 328→ }, 329→ }, 330→}); 331→``` 332→ 333→### createAsyncSlice 334→ 335→```typescript 336→const usersSlice = createAsyncSlice({ 337→ id: 'users', 338→ cache: 'session', 339→ context: { page: 1 }, // Required context for load function 340→ initial: { value: [], loading: false }, // Optional initial override 341→ load: async (context) => { 342→ const response = await fetch(`/api/users?page=${context.page}`); 343→ return response.json(); 344→ }, 345→}); 346→ 347→// Async slices automatically generate these actions: 348→// - load: Start loading (sets loading=true) 349→// - success: Set value on success 350→// - fail: Set error on failure 351→// - clear: Reset to initial state 352→``` 353→ 354→### Async Slice State Shape 355→ 356→```typescript 357→type AsyncSliceState = { 358→ loading: boolean; 359→ loaded: boolean; 360→ failed: boolean; 361→ error: string | null; 362→ value: T | null; 363→ changes: number; 364→}; 365→``` 366→ 367→### Cache Strategies 368→ 369→| Strategy | Description | 370→|----------|-------------| 371→| `'session'` | Invalidate when sessionID changes | 372→| `'user'` | Invalidate when userID changes | 373→| `'global'` | Never automatically invalidate | 374→ 375→### Machine API 376→ 377→```typescript 378→const machine = createMachine({ id: 'app', slices: { ... } }); 379→ 380→// Dispatch actions 381→machine.dispatch(machine.actions.counter.increment()); 382→machine.dispatch(machine.actions.counter.add(5)); 383→ 384→// Get current state 385→const state = machine.getState(); 386→ 387→// Subscribe to changes 388→const unsubscribe = machine.subscribe((state, prevState) => { 389→ console.log('State changed:', state); 390→}); 391→ 392→// Reset to initial state 393→machine.reset(); 394→ 395→// Access action creators and types 396→machine.actions.counter.increment(); // => { type: '@app/counter/increment', payload: undefined } 397→machine.actionTypes.counter.increment; // => '@app/counter/increment' 398→ 399→// Context management 400→machine.getContext(); // => { userID: 'user-123' } 401→machine.provideContext({ userID: 'new-user' }); 402→ 403→// Direct reducer access (for Redux integration) 404→const rootReducer = machine.reducer; 405→``` 406→ 407→### React Hooks 408→ 409→```typescript 410→// In a React component 411→function Counter() { 412→ // useMachine - Get actions and full state 413→ const { state, actions, dispatch } = machine.useMachine(); 414→ 415→ // useSelector - Select specific state slice 416→ const count = machine.useSelector((s) => s.counter.count); 417→ const doubled = machine.useSelector((s) => s.doubleCount); // From selectors 418→ 419→ return ( 420→
421→

Count: {count}

422→ 423→
424→ ); 425→} 426→ 427→// Named exports for specific machine 428→const { useApp, useAppSelector } = machine; 429→// useApp() and useAppSelector() are aliases for useMachine/useSelector 430→``` 431→ 432→--- 433→ 434→## Helpers 435→ 436→### Error Utilities 437→ 438→```typescript 439→import { 440→ InvariantError, 441→ invariant, 442→ tryCatch, 443→ moveStackTrace, 444→ createErrorList 445→} from '@belt/toolkit'; 446→ 447→// invariant - Assert truthy or throw 448→invariant(user, 'User is required'); 449→invariant(user.id, (typeName) => `Expected id, got ${typeName}`); 450→ 451→// tryCatch - Safe execution returning tuple 452→const [error, result] = tryCatch(() => JSON.parse(data)); 453→if (error) { 454→ console.error('Parse failed:', error.message); 455→} else { 456→ console.log('Parsed:', result); 457→} 458→ 459→// InvariantError - Custom error with clean stack traces 460→throw new InvariantError('Invalid input', myFunction, { debug: data }); 461→ 462→// moveStackTrace - Clean error origins 463→function validate(data: unknown) { 464→ return moveStackTrace(() => { 465→ if (!data) throw new Error('Data required'); 466→ return data; 467→ }, validate); // Error points to validate() caller 468→} 469→ 470→// createErrorList - Collect multiple errors 471→const errors = createErrorList('Validation failed: '); 472→if (!name) errors.add('name is required'); 473→if (!email) errors.add('email is required'); 474→errors.check(); // Throws if any errors added 475→``` 476→ 477→### Object Utilities 478→ 479→```typescript 480→import { 481→ getProperty, 482→ setProperty, 483→ objectKeys, 484→ objectEntries, 485→ objectValues, 486→ isObject, 487→ assertObject, 488→ keyBy, 489→ reduceObject, 490→ requireAll, 491→ deepFreeze, 492→} from '@belt/toolkit'; 493→ 494→// getProperty - Type-safe deep access 495→const user = { profile: { name: 'John', age: 30 } }; 496→const name = getProperty(user, 'profile.name'); // 'John' (throws if falsy) 497→const age = getProperty(user, 'profile.age', true); // 30 (allows falsy) 498→ 499→// setProperty - Type-safe deep set 500→setProperty(user, 'profile.name', 'Jane'); 501→ 502→// Typed object iteration 503→const obj = { a: 1, b: 2 }; 504→objectKeys(obj); // ('a' | 'b')[] 505→objectEntries(obj); // ['a', 1] | ['b', 2][] 506→objectValues(obj); // number[] 507→ 508→// isObject / assertObject 509→if (isObject(data)) { /* data is Record */ } 510→assertObject(data); // throws if not plain object 511→ 512→// keyBy - Create lookup from array 513→const users = [{ id: 1, name: 'A' }, { id: 2, name: 'B' }]; 514→const byId = keyBy(users, 'id'); // { 1: {...}, 2: {...} } 515→const byName = keyBy(users, (u) => u.name); // { A: {...}, B: {...} } 516→ 517→// reduceObject - Reduce over entries 518→const doubled = reduceObject({ a: 1, b: 2 }, ([k, v]) => ({ [k]: v * 2 })); 519→// => { a: 2, b: 4 } 520→ 521→// requireAll - Assert all properties are truthy 522→const config = requireAll({ host, port, db }); // throws if any falsy 523→ 524→// deepFreeze - Recursively freeze object 525→const frozen = deepFreeze({ nested: { value: 1 } }); 526→``` 527→ 528→### Type Utilities 529→ 530→```typescript 531→import { stringEnum } from '@belt/toolkit'; 532→import type { 533→ AnyRecord, 534→ UnknownRecord, 535→ Simplify, 536→ Merge, 537→ Paths, 538→ PathType, 539→ PromiseValue, 540→ MaybePromise, 541→ MaybeArray, 542→ Writable, 543→ PartialFields, 544→ UnionToIntersection, 545→ Primitive, 546→ Serializable, 547→ AnyFunction, 548→ IsAny, 549→ IsNever, 550→ IsOptional, 551→ Cast, 552→ Join, 553→} from '@belt/toolkit'; 554→ 555→// stringEnum - Create typed string array for enums 556→const Status = stringEnum('pending', 'active', 'done'); 557→type StatusType = (typeof Status)[number]; // 'pending' | 'active' | 'done' 558→ 559→// Simplify - Flatten intersection types for readability 560→type Flat = Simplify<{ a: 1 } & { b: 2 }>; // { a: 1; b: 2 } 561→ 562→// Merge - Deep merge two types 563→type Merged = Merge<{ a: 1; b: 2 }, { b: 3; c: 4 }>; // { a: 1; b: 3; c: 4 } 564→ 565→// Paths - Get all valid paths of an object 566→type UserPaths = Paths<{ user: { name: string } }>; // 'user' | 'user.name' 567→ 568→// PathType - Get type at path 569→type NameType = PathType<{ user: { name: string } }, 'user.name'>; // string 570→ 571→// PromiseValue - Extract resolved type 572→type Value = PromiseValue>; // string 573→ 574→// MaybePromise - T | Promise 575→type MaybeString = MaybePromise; // string | Promise 576→ 577→// MaybeArray - T | T[] 578→type MaybeStrings = MaybeArray; // string | string[] 579→ 580→// Writable - Remove readonly modifiers 581→type Mutable = Writable<{ readonly a: 1 }>; // { a: 1 } 582→ 583→// PartialFields - Make specific fields optional 584→type Partial = PartialFields<{ a: 1; b: 2; c: 3 }, ['a', 'b']>; 585→// { a?: 1; b?: 2; c: 3 } 586→ 587→// UnionToIntersection 588→type Inter = UnionToIntersection<{ a: 1 } | { b: 2 }>; // { a: 1 } & { b: 2 } 589→ 590→// Primitive types 591→type P = Primitive; // string | number | boolean | null | undefined | bigint | symbol 592→ 593→// Join - Join array as string type 594→type Joined = Join<['a', 'b', 'c'], '.'>; // 'a.b.c' 595→``` 596→ 597→### Time Utilities 598→ 599→```typescript 600→import { ms } from '@belt/toolkit'; 601→ 602→// Convert time strings to milliseconds 603→ms('1s'); // 1000 604→ms('5m'); // 300000 605→ms('2h'); // 7200000 606→ms('1d'); // 86400000 607→ms('1w'); // 604800000 608→ms('1mo'); // ~2629746000 (1 month) 609→ms('1y'); // ~31556952000 (1 year) 610→ 611→// Also accepts numbers (treated as ms) 612→ms(1000); // 1000 613→ 614→// Type-safe time strings 615→import type { MSTimeString } from '@belt/toolkit'; 616→const timeout: MSTimeString = '30s'; 617→``` 618→ 619→### Other Utilities 620→ 621→```typescript 622→import { 623→ getTypeOf, 624→ maskData, 625→ delay, 626→ createProxy, 627→ formatDate, 628→ setDefaultDateLocale, 629→ getDefaultDateLocale, 630→ EMPTY 631→} from '@belt/toolkit'; 632→ 633→// getTypeOf - Get readable type name 634→getTypeOf(null); // 'Null' 635→getTypeOf(NaN); // 'NaN' 636→getTypeOf(new Date()); // 'Date' 637→getTypeOf([]); // 'Array' 638→getTypeOf({}); // 'Object' 639→ 640→// maskData - Mask values for safe logging 641→maskData({ password: 'secret', user: { email: 'a@b.com' } }); 642→// => { password: 'String', user: { email: 'String' } } 643→ 644→// delay - Promise-based setTimeout 645→await delay(1000); // Wait 1 second 646→await delay(); // Default 500ms 647→ 648→// createProxy - Lazy initialization 649→const heavyObject = createProxy(() => { 650→ console.log('Initialized!'); 651→ return computeExpensiveValue(); 652→}); 653→// 'Initialized!' only logs on first access 654→ 655→// formatDate - Format dates with optional locale 656→formatDate(new Date(), 'yyyy-MM-dd'); // '2025-12-15' 657→formatDate('2025-09-25', 'MMM dd'); // 'Sep 25' 658→ 659→// Set global default locale 660→import { ptBR } from 'date-fns/locale'; 661→setDefaultDateLocale(ptBR); 662→formatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM'); // 'dezembro' 663→ 664→// Override locale for single call 665→import { enUS } from 'date-fns/locale'; 666→formatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM', { locale: enUS }); // 'December' 667→ 668→// Get current default locale 669→getDefaultDateLocale(); // Locale | null 670→ 671→// EMPTY - Unique symbol for empty/unset values 672→const cache = new Map(); 673→cache.set('key', EMPTY); // Distinguish from undefined 674→``` 675→ 676→--- 677→ 678→## License 679→ 680→MIT 681→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1→# React + TypeScript + Vite
     2→
     3→This template provides a minimal setup to get React working in Vite with HMR and some ESLint rules.
     4→

---

### Astro + React Integration

#### Key Learning
Astro's React integration requires specific hydration directives for client-only rendering.

#### Important Configuration
```astro
<!-- For heavy React components like R3F Canvas -->
<PlanningView client:only="react" />
```

#### Common Pitfalls
- Don't use `client:load` for R3F - it will cause SSR issues
- `client:only="react"` completely skips server rendering
- Ensure React 19 compatibility with all libraries

---

### Three.js Shaders (GLSL)

input.old_string:
#### CRITICAL: Running Scripts in Monorepos

**The root `bun run dev` may fail** if the filter doesn't match the package name:

```bash
# Root package.json uses filter:
"dev": "bun --bun run --filter '@mms/app-system' dev"

# The filter MUST match the 'name' field in apps/system/package.json:
"name": "@mms/app-system"  # ← This is what the filter matches
```

**If root scripts fail, ALWAYS try the direct approach:**

```bash
# This ALWAYS works:
cd apps/system && bun run dev

# Or check what the package is actually named:
cat apps/system/package.json | grep '"name"'
```

#### Common Pitfalls
- **Never assume root scripts work** - verify first with `./init.sh --no-server`
- Root scripts use filters that must match exact package names
- Always use `bun add` from the correct directory
- Cross-package imports need proper exports in package.json
- Run `bun i` from root after adding new packages

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Notes for Claude - Future Reference This file contains learnings, gotchas, and solutions discovered during development. Read this to avoid repeating past mistakes. --- ## Technology-Specific Notes ### React Three Fiber (R3F) #### Key Learning R3F v9 has significant API changes from v8. Always check the latest documentation. #### Common Pitfalls - Never use `useFrame` outside of Canvas context - `Html` component from drei requires `transform` prop for 3D positioning - InstancedMesh color changes require `needsUpdate = true` on the attribute #### Correct Usage ```typescript // Correct way to update InstancedMesh colors mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true; // Correct Html positioning in 3D
Content
``` --- ### Astro + React Integration #### Key Learning Astro's React integration requires specific hydration directives for client-only rendering. #### Important Configuration ```astro ``` #### Common Pitfalls - Don't use `client:load` for R3F - it will cause SSR issues - `client:only="react"` completely skips server rendering - Ensure React 19 compatibility with all libraries --- ### Three.js Shaders (GLSL) #### Key Learning Fragment shaders for heatmaps need careful color interpolation. #### Correct Usage ```glsl // Color ramp for heatmap (Yellow → Orange → Red) vec3 ramp(float t) { vec3 a = vec3(1.0, 0.95, 0.5); // light yellow vec3 b = vec3(1.0, 0.6, 0.0); // orange vec3 c = vec3(0.85, 0.0, 0.0); // red return mix(mix(a, b, smoothstep(0.0, 0.5, t)), c, smoothstep(0.5, 1.0, t)); } ``` --- ### Bun Workspaces / Monorepo #### Key Learning Bun workspaces require proper package.json structure and cross-package imports. #### Important Configuration ```json { "workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"] } ``` #### CRITICAL: Running Scripts in Monorepos **The root `bun run dev` may fail** if the filter doesn't match the package name: ```bash # Root package.json uses filter: "dev": "bun --bun run --filter '@mms/app-system' dev" # The filter MUST match the 'name' field in apps/system/package.json: "name": "@mms/app-system" # ← This is what the filter matches ``` **If root scripts fail, ALWAYS try the direct approach:** ```bash # This ALWAYS works: cd apps/system && bun run dev # Or check what the package is actually named: cat apps/system/package.json | grep '"name"' ``` #### Common Pitfalls - **Never assume root scripts work** - verify first with `./init.sh --no-server` - Root scripts use filters that must match exact package names - Always use `bun add` from the correct directory - Cross-package imports need proper exports in package.json - Run `bun i` from root after adding new packages --- ## Package/Library Notes ### @react-three/drei #### IMPORTANT: Html Component **Correct import**: `import { Html } from '@react-three/drei'` #### Why this matters The Html component bridges WebGL and DOM, allowing real HTML elements to be positioned in 3D space. #### Correct usage ```tsx
Clickable content
``` --- ### three-stdlib #### IMPORTANT: Line2 for Fat Lines **Correct import**: `import { Line2, LineGeometry, LineMaterial } from 'three-stdlib'` #### Why this matters Standard Three.js Line has 1px width limit. Line2 supports variable width. #### Correct usage ```typescript const geometry = new LineGeometry(); geometry.setPositions([x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, z2]); const material = new LineMaterial({ color: 0x3b82f6, linewidth: 2, resolution: new Vector2(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight) }); ``` --- ## Error Solutions ### Error: "No packages matched the filter" (Bun monorepo) **Cause**: Root script uses `--filter` with wrong package name **Solution**: 1. Check actual package name: `cat apps/system/package.json | grep '"name"'` 2. Run directly in the app folder: `cd apps/system && bun run dev` 3. Or fix the filter in root package.json to match the actual package name ### Error: "Cannot use import statement outside a module" **Cause**: Package trying to use CommonJS in ESM context **Solution**: Ensure `"type": "module"` in package.json, use proper imports ### Error: "useFrame must be called inside Canvas" **Cause**: React hook called outside R3F context **Solution**: Move component inside `` or pass state via props/context ### Error: "THREE.WebGLRenderer: Context Lost" **Cause**: GPU memory exhausted or too many textures **Solution**: Dispose unused textures, reduce DataTexture size, check for memory leaks ### Error: InstancedMesh colors not updating **Cause**: Buffer attribute not marked for update **Solution**: Set `mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true` after changing colors --- ## General Rules 1. **Always research official documentation** before trying to resolve import/ESM errors 2. **Check for alternative versions** of packages for different bundlers 3. **Don't remove dependencies** the user explicitly requested - find the correct way to use them 4. **Test after every change** - don't batch multiple changes 5. **Commit frequently** - small, focused commits are better than large ones 6. **Use bun add** - never manually edit package.json for dependencies --- ## Project-Specific Gotchas ### LOD State Machine The LOD (Level of Detail) is controlled by zoom level: - `zoom < 0.5` → 'macro' (shader heatmap) - `0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0` → 'meso' (InstancedMesh) - `zoom > 2.0` → 'micro' (HTML overlay) ### Virtual Scroll Sync Sidebar scroll is synced with camera Y using: ```typescript const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24; // DOM → Camera const y = -el.scrollTop / PX_PER_TRAIN; // Camera → DOM el.scrollTop = -viewport.y * PX_PER_TRAIN; ``` ### Spatial Hash Grid MesoLayer uses spatial hashing for O(1) instance picking: - Grid cell size matches task dimensions - Hash key: `${Math.floor(x / cellSize)}_${Math.floor(y / cellSize)}` --- ## Useful Commands ```bash # Install all workspace dependencies bun i # Start development server bun run dev # Type check all packages bun run typecheck # Build for production bun run build # Check git status git status # View recent commits git --no-pager log --oneline -10 ``` --- ## Resources - [React Three Fiber Docs](https://docs.pmnd.rs/react-three-fiber) - [Drei Docs](https://github.com/pmndrs/drei) - [Three.js Docs](https://threejs.org/docs/) - [Astro Docs](https://docs.astro.build) - [Bun Docs](https://bun.sh/docs) - [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/docs)

// DOM → Camera const y = -el.scrollTop / PX_PER_TRAIN; // Camera → DOM el.scrollTop = -viewport.y * PX_PER_TRAIN; ``` ### Spatial Hash Grid MesoLayer uses spatial hashing for O(1) instance picking: - Grid cell size matches task dimensions - Hash key: `${Math.floor(x / cellSize)}_${Math.floor(y / cellSize)}` --- ## Useful Commands ```bash # Install all workspace dependencies bun i # Start development server bun run dev # Type check all packages bun run typecheck # Build for production bun run build # Check git status git status # View recent commits git --no-pager log --oneline -10 ``` --- ## Resources - [React Three Fiber Docs](https://docs.pmnd.rs/react-three-fiber) - [Drei Docs](https://github.com/pmndrs/drei) - [Three.js Docs](https://threejs.org/docs/) - [Astro Docs](https://docs.astro.build) - [Bun Docs](https://bun.sh/docs) - [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/docs)

# Notes for Claude - Future Reference This file contains learnings, gotchas, and solutions discovered during development. Read this to avoid repeating past mistakes. --- ## Technology-Specific Notes ### React Three Fiber (R3F) #### Key Learning R3F v9 has significant API changes from v8. Always check the latest documentation. #### Common Pitfalls - Never use `useFrame` outside of Canvas context - `Html` component from drei requires `transform` prop for 3D positioning - InstancedMesh color changes require `needsUpdate = true` on the attribute #### Correct Usage ```typescript // Correct way to update InstancedMesh colors mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true; // Correct Html positioning in 3D
Content
``` --- ### Three.js Shaders (GLSL) #### Key Learning Fragment shaders for heatmaps need careful color interpolation. #### Correct Usage ```glsl // Color ramp for heatmap (Yellow → Orange → Red) vec3 ramp(float t) { vec3 a = vec3(1.0, 0.95, 0.5); // light yellow vec3 b = vec3(1.0, 0.6, 0.0); // orange vec3 c = vec3(0.85, 0.0, 0.0); // red return mix(mix(a, b, smoothstep(0.0, 0.5, t)), c, smoothstep(0.5, 1.0, t)); } ``` --- ### Bun Workspaces / Monorepo #### Key Learning Bun workspaces require proper package.json structure and cross-package imports. #### Important Configuration ```json { "workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"] } ``` #### CRITICAL: Running Scripts in Monorepos **If root scripts fail, try the direct approach:** ```bash # This ALWAYS works: cd apps/canvas && bun run dev # Or check what the package is actually named: cat apps/canvas/package.json | grep '"name"' ``` #### Common Pitfalls - **Never assume root scripts work** - verify first with `./init.sh --no-server` - Root scripts use filters that must match exact package names - Always use `bun add` from the correct directory - Cross-package imports need proper exports in package.json - Run `bun i` from root after adding new packages --- ## Package/Library Notes ### @react-three/drei #### IMPORTANT: Html Component **Correct import**: `import { Html } from '@react-three/drei'` #### Why this matters The Html component bridges WebGL and DOM, allowing real HTML elements to be positioned in 3D space. #### Correct usage ```tsx
Clickable content
``` --- ### three-stdlib #### IMPORTANT: Line2 for Fat Lines **Correct import**: `import { Line2, LineGeometry, LineMaterial } from 'three-stdlib'` #### Why this matters Standard Three.js Line has 1px width limit. Line2 supports variable width. #### Correct usage ```typescript const geometry = new LineGeometry(); geometry.setPositions([x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, z2]); const material = new LineMaterial({ color: 0x3b82f6, linewidth: 2, resolution: new Vector2(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight) }); ``` --- ## Error Solutions ### Error: "No packages matched the filter" (Bun monorepo) **Cause**: Root script uses `--filter` with wrong package name **Solution**: 1. Check actual package name: `cat apps/system/package.json | grep '"name"'` 2. Run directly in the app folder: `cd apps/system && bun run dev` 3. Or fix the filter in root package.json to match the actual package name ### Error: "Cannot use import statement outside a module" **Cause**: Package trying to use CommonJS in ESM context **Solution**: Ensure `"type": "module"` in package.json, use proper imports ### Error: "useFrame must be called inside Canvas" **Cause**: React hook called outside R3F context **Solution**: Move component inside `` or pass state via props/context ### Error: "THREE.WebGLRenderer: Context Lost" **Cause**: GPU memory exhausted or too many textures **Solution**: Dispose unused textures, reduce DataTexture size, check for memory leaks ### Error: InstancedMesh colors not updating **Cause**: Buffer attribute not marked for update **Solution**: Set `mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true` after changing colors --- ## General Rules 1. **Always research official documentation** before trying to resolve import/ESM errors 2. **Check for alternative versions** of packages for different bundlers 3. **Don't remove dependencies** the user explicitly requested - find the correct way to use them 4. **Test after every change** - don't batch multiple changes 5. **Commit frequently** - small, focused commits are better than large ones 6. **Use bun add** - never manually edit package.json for dependencies --- ## Project-Specific Gotchas ### LOD State Machine The LOD (Level of Detail) is controlled by zoom level: - `zoom < 0.5` → 'macro' (shader heatmap) - `0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0` → 'meso' (InstancedMesh) - `zoom > 2.0` → 'micro' (HTML overlay) ### Virtual Scroll Sync Sidebar scroll is synced with camera Y using: ```typescript const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24; // DOM → Camera const y = -el.scrollTop / PX_PER_TRAIN; // Camera → DOM el.scrollTop = -viewport.y * PX_PER_TRAIN; ``` ### Spatial Hash Grid MesoLayer uses spatial hashing for O(1) instance picking: - Grid cell size matches task dimensions - Hash key: `${Math.floor(x / cellSize)}_${Math.floor(y / cellSize)}` --- ## Useful Commands ```bash # Install all workspace dependencies bun i # Start development server bun run dev # Type check all packages bun run typecheck # Build for production bun run build # Check git status git status # View recent commits git --no-pager log --oneline -10 ``` --- ## Resources - [React Three Fiber Docs](https://docs.pmnd.rs/react-three-fiber) - [Drei Docs](https://github.com/pmndrs/drei) - [Three.js Docs](https://threejs.org/docs/) - [Astro Docs](https://docs.astro.build) - [Bun Docs](https://bun.sh/docs) - [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/docs)

# Visual Design Specifications

This directory contains the **Pixel-Perfect Target** visual references for the Metro Integrated Planning System. Each image represents a specific view or component that must be replicated with high fidelity.

## View Mapping

### Macro View (Strategic Planning: 10-20 years)
**Implementation:** `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MacroLayer.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-1.png` | Dense grid/heatmap showing fleet density and "health" via color gradients (Yellow → Orange → Red) |

**Technical Requirements:**
- Single `PlaneGeometry` covering visible area
- Fragment Shader (GLSL) receiving `DataTexture`
- Color ramp: Yellow (low density) → Orange → Red (high density)
- Goal: 1 Draw Call, native GPU performance

---

### Meso View (Tactical Planning: 2-5 years)
**Implementation:** `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-2.png` | Colorful diagonal strips (DNA-like pattern) - high-density individual allocations |
| `screen-4.png` | Dense diagonal allocation pattern with interleaved colors |
| `screen-5.png` | Color-coded resource allocation blocks |

**Technical Requirements:**
- `InstancedMesh` with up to 60,000 instances
- Colors controlled via `InstancedBufferAttribute`
- Spatial Hash Grid for hover detection
- Color palette: Deep Blue (routine), Orange (overhaul)

---

### Micro View (Operational Planning: 4-8 weeks)
**Implementation:** `packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MicroLayer.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-3.jpg` | Text boxes, alert icons, arrows showing task dependencies |
| `screen-6.png` | Task detail cards with conflict indicators (red alerts) |

**Technical Requirements:**
- `<Html>` component from drei with `transform` for task cards
- `Line2` (Fat Lines) for dependency arrows (smooth Bezier curves)
- Full Tailwind styling (shadows, rounded corners, flexbox)
- Accessibility: keyboard focus (tabIndex), native click events
- Conflict indicator: red border + warning icon

---

### UI Layout Reference
**Implementation:** `apps/system/src/components/PlanningView.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-7.png` | Header bar + left sidebar (Train IDs) layout |

**Technical Requirements:**
- Header: Fleet availability %, budget status
- Left sidebar: Scrollable train list synchronized with canvas Y position
- Footer: Time scrubber / Minimap controlling canvas X position

---

## Color Palette

| Element | Color | Hex |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Background | Soft corporate gray | `#f4f4f5` (zinc-100) |
| Task (routine) | Deep Blue | `#1d4ed8` (blue-700) |
| Task (overhaul) | Orange | `#f59e0b` (amber-500) |
| Alert/Conflict | Danger Red | `#dc2626` (red-600) |
| Dependency lines | Slate | `#64748b` (slate-500) |

---

## Implementation Checklist

- [x] MacroLayer: Procedural shader heatmap
- [x] MesoLayer: InstancedMesh with spatial hashing
- [x] MicroLayer: HTML overlay + Line2 curves
- [x] Virtual scroll sync (sidebar ↔ canvas)
- [x] Time scrubber minimap
- [ ] Pixel-perfect color matching
- [ ] Fine-tuning visual density patterns

| `screen-1.png` | Dense grid/heatmap showing fleet density and "health" via color gradients (Yellow → Orange → Red) |

**Technical Requirements:**
- Single `PlaneGeometry` covering visible area
- Fragment Shader (GLSL) receiving `DataTexture`
- Color ramp: Yellow (low density) → Orange → Red (high density)
- Goal: 1 Draw Call, native GPU performance

---

### Meso View (Tactical Planning: 2-5 years)
**Implementation:** `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-2.png` | Colorful diagonal strips (DNA-like pattern) - high-density individual allocations |
| `screen-4.png` | Dense diagonal allocation pattern with interleaved colors |
| `screen-5.png` | Color-coded resource allocation blocks |

**Technical Requirements:**
- `InstancedMesh` with up to 60,000 instances
- Colors controlled via `InstancedBufferAttribute`
- Spatial Hash Grid for hover detection
- Color palette: Deep Blue (routine), Orange (overhaul)

---

### Micro View (Operational Planning: 4-8 weeks)
**Implementation:** `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-3.jpg` | Text boxes, alert icons, arrows showing task dependencies |
| `screen-6.png` | Task detail cards with conflict indicators (red alerts) |

**Technical Requirements:**
- `<Html>` component from drei with `transform` for task cards
- `Line2` (Fat Lines) for dependency arrows (smooth Bezier curves)
- Full Tailwind styling (shadows, rounded corners, flexbox)
- Accessibility: keyboard focus (tabIndex), native click events
- Conflict indicator: red border + warning icon

---

### UI Layout Reference
**Implementation:** `apps/system/src/components/PlanningView.tsx`

| Reference | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `screen-7.png` | Header bar + left sidebar (Train IDs) layout |

**Technical Requirements:**
- Header: Fleet availability %, budget status
- Left sidebar: Scrollable train list synchronized with canvas Y position
- Footer: Time scrubber / Minimap controlling canvas X position

---

## Color Palette

| Element | Color | Hex |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Background | Soft corporate gray | `#f4f4f5` (zinc-100) |
| Task (routine) | Deep Blue | `#1d4ed8` (blue-700) |
| Task (overhaul) | Orange | `#f59e0b` (amber-500) |
| Alert/Conflict | Danger Red | `#dc2626` (red-600) |
| Dependency lines | Slate | `#64748b` (slate-500) |

---

## Implementation Checklist

- [x] MacroLayer: Procedural shader heatmap
- [x] MesoLayer: InstancedMesh with spatial hashing
- [x] MicroLayer: HTML overlay + Line2 curves
- [x] Virtual scroll sync (sidebar ↔ canvas)
- [x] Time scrubber minimap
- [ ] Pixel-perfect color matching
- [ ] Fine-tuning visual density patterns
